framework.name: Agent Payments Protocol (AP2)
framework.description: Open-standard protocol extending A2A and MCP that uses Verifiable Credentials (cryptographically signed Mandates) for agent authorization in e-commerce. Implements role-based architecture with separate Shopping Agents, Credentials Providers, Merchant Endpoints, and Payment Processors. Defines three mandate types: Cart Mandate (human-present), Intent Mandate (human-not-present with TTL/budget constraints), and Payment Mandate (issuer-ready with AI risk signals).
framework.raw_quote: The Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) [1], an open extension to Agent2Agent (A2A) [2] and Model Context Protocol (MCP) [3], introduces Verifiable Credentials (VCs) in the form of cryptographically signed Mandates to establish trust without relying solely on probabilistic AI inferences.

source.title: Secure Use of the Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) | CSA
